policy: Public .gov / official statute sources only; HTTP 403/SPA/Cloudflare = park; gaps explicit.

sources.name: eCFR — 45 CFR Part 164 (Security and Privacy)
sources.reliability_note: Authoritative codified HIPAA Privacy/Security/Breach rules; preferred over HHS guidance pages when those are inaccessible.

sources.name: eCFR — 45 CFR §160.103 (HIPAA Definitions)
sources.reliability_note: Covered entity, business associate, PHI, ePHI definitions.

sources.name: eCFR — 45 CFR §164.502 (Privacy general rules)
sources.reliability_note: Permitted/prohibited uses; minimum necessary; BA use limits.

sources.name: eCFR — 45 CFR §164.504 (Organizational / BAA requirements)
sources.reliability_note: §164.504(e) BAA contract elements and subcontractor chain.

sources.name: eCFR — 45 CFR §164.308 (Administrative safeguards)
sources.reliability_note: Risk analysis, workforce security, incident response, contingency plan.

sources.name: eCFR — 45 CFR §164.312 (Technical safeguards)
sources.reliability_note: Access control, audit, integrity, authentication, transmission security.

sources.name: eCFR — 45 CFR §164.314 (Organizational requirements — Security Rule)
sources.reliability_note: BA written contract / other arrangement for ePHI safeguards.

sources.name: eCFR — 45 CFR §164.404 (Breach notification — individuals)
sources.reliability_note: 60-day individual notice; content and methods.

sources.name: eCFR — 45 CFR §164.530 (Privacy administrative requirements)
sources.reliability_note: Privacy official, training, safeguards, complaints, 6-year documentation.

sources.name: California Civil Code §56.10 (CMIA disclosure)
sources.reliability_note: Authorization-first disclosure; HIPAA cross-reference at §56.10(c)(21)-(22).

sources.name: California Civil Code §56.11 (CMIA authorization form)
sources.reliability_note: 14-point type, specific uses, 1-year max expiration (exceptions noted).

sources.name: NY Department of Health — HIPAA regulations hub
sources.reliability_note: State DOH HIPAA resources; preemption charts; NPP links.

sources.name: NY DOH — HIPAA Preemption Charts (PHL §17, §18)
sources.reliability_note: Agency analysis of when NY Public Health Law prevails over HIPAA Privacy Rule.

sources.name: HHS.gov — HIPAA for Professionals (Privacy)
sources.reliability_note: HTTP 403 at harvest; use eCFR Part 164 as primary until portal accessible.

sources.name: HHS.gov — HIPAA Security Rule guidance
sources.reliability_note: HTTP 403 at harvest; eCFR Subpart C fallback.

sources.name: HHS.gov — Business Associates
sources.reliability_note: HTTP 403; BAA elements sourced from §164.504(e) and §164.314.

sources.name: Texas Statutes — Health & Safety Code Ch. 181 (Medical Records Privacy) index
sources.reliability_note: Chapter anchor confirmed; section text not harvestable (SPA/JS shell, no statute body in HTML).

sources.name: N.Y. Gen. Bus. Law §899-aa (SHIELD Act)
sources.reliability_note: Cloudflare block at harvest; statute text NOT FOUND in lane — REQUIRES_CONFIRMATION via alternate primary.

sources.name: NY DFS — Cybersecurity Resource Center (23 NYCRR 500 / SHIELD context)
sources.reliability_note: Portal live (200) but sparse text harvest; DFS Part 500 applies to covered financial entities — not all medical practices.

sources.name: NY Attorney General — Data breach reporting
sources.reliability_note: URL 404 at harvest; SHIELD breach notification facts require alternate source.

sources.name: Texas Medical Board — Privacy and Security
sources.reliability_note: Page 404; Texas medical-practice privacy add-ons rely on HS Ch. 181 when text accessible.
